concepts.name: Limit
concepts.short_definition: The value a function approaches as its input approaches a certain value.

concepts.name: Continuity
concepts.short_definition: A function is continuous if its graph can be drawn without lifting the pen.

concepts.name: Instantaneous Rate of Change
concepts.short_definition: The rate at which a quantity changes at a specific point in time.

concepts.name: Average Rate of Change
concepts.short_definition: The rate of change of a quantity over an interval.

concepts.name: Tangent Line
concepts.short_definition: A line that touches a curve at a single point and has the same slope as the curve at that point.

concepts.name: Derivative
concepts.short_definition: The instantaneous rate of change of a function, or the slope of the tangent line.

concepts.name: Derivative Notation
concepts.short_definition: Symbols used to represent the derivative of a function.

concepts.name: Power Rule
concepts.short_definition: A rule for finding the derivative of a power function.

concepts.name: Constant Rule
concepts.short_definition: The derivative of a constant function is zero.

concepts.name: Constant Multiple Rule
concepts.short_definition: The derivative of a constant times a function is the constant times the derivative of the function.

concepts.name: Sum Rule
concepts.short_definition: The derivative of a sum of functions is the sum of their derivatives.

concepts.name: Difference Rule
concepts.short_definition: The derivative of a difference of functions is the difference of their derivatives.

concepts.name: Product Rule
concepts.short_definition: A rule for finding the derivative of the product of two functions.

concepts.name: Quotient Rule
concepts.short_definition: A rule for finding the derivative of the quotient of two functions.

concepts.name: Chain Rule
concepts.short_definition: A rule for finding the derivative of a composite function.

concepts.name: Implicit Differentiation
concepts.short_definition: A technique for finding the derivative of an implicitly defined function.

concepts.name: Higher-Order Derivatives
concepts.short_definition: Derivatives of derivatives.

concepts.name: Second Derivative
concepts.short_definition: The derivative of the first derivative.

concepts.name: Concavity
concepts.short_definition: The curvature of a function's graph, determined by the second derivative.

concepts.name: Inflection Point
concepts.short_definition: A point on a curve where the concavity changes.

concepts.name: Critical Point
concepts.short_definition: A point where the derivative is zero or undefined.

concepts.name: Local Extrema
concepts.short_definition: The highest or lowest points in a neighborhood of a function.

concepts.name: First Derivative Test
concepts.short_definition: A test using the first derivative to find local extrema.

concepts.name: Second Derivative Test
concepts.short_definition: A test using the second derivative to find local extrema.

concepts.name: Absolute Extrema
concepts.short_definition: The highest or lowest points of a function over its entire domain.

concepts.name: Optimization
concepts.short_definition: Finding the maximum or minimum value of a function.

concepts.name: Related Rates
concepts.short_definition: Problems involving rates of change of multiple related variables.

concepts.name: L'Hôpital's Rule
concepts.short_definition: A rule for evaluating limits of indeterminate forms.

concepts.name: Antiderivative
concepts.short_definition: A function whose derivative is a given function.

concepts.name: Indefinite Integral
concepts.short_definition: The set of all antiderivatives of a function.

concepts.name: Integral Notation
concepts.short_definition: Symbols used to represent integration.

concepts.name: Power Rule for Integration
concepts.short_definition: A rule for finding the antiderivative of a power function.

concepts.name: Constant Rule for Integration
concepts.short_definition: The antiderivative of a constant function.

concepts.name: Sum Rule for Integration
concepts.short_definition: The antiderivative of a sum of functions is the sum of their antiderivatives.

concepts.name: Difference Rule for Integration
concepts.short_definition: The antiderivative of a difference of functions is the difference of their antiderivatives.

concepts.name: Constant Multiple Rule for Integration
concepts.short_definition: The antiderivative of a constant times a function is the constant times the antiderivative of the function.

concepts.name: Integration by Substitution
concepts.short_definition: A technique for finding antiderivatives of composite functions.

concepts.name: Definite Integral
concepts.short_definition: The net area under the curve of a function between two points.

concepts.name: Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
concepts.short_definition: Connects differentiation and integration.
